summary: Reviewed `git diff main...HEAD`, `git log --oneline main..HEAD`, and `reviews/zoom-first-light.md`. The core diagnostics probe mostly matches the requested shape, but the branch also adds unconditional client telemetry that violates the mock-mode/no-touch acceptance criterion. I attempted `npm test` and `npm run build`; both were blocked by the read-only sandbox because Vite/Vitest need to write temporary config files.

findings.title: Unconditional telemetry changes mock-mode behavior
findings.claim: The spec requires the diagnostic behavior to run only when `VITE_USE_ZOOM === '1'` and `?diag=1`, with default mock dev behavior unchanged. This new inline script runs on every page load regardless of those flags, installs global `window.onerror`/`onunhandledrejection` handlers, monkey-patches `console.log`/`warn`/`error`, and immediately posts `telemetry initialized` plus future console output to `/api/log`. That is observable behavior in default mock mode and violates AC4.
findings.suggestion: Remove this global HTML telemetry hook, or gate it behind the same Zoom diagnostics trigger and keep diagnostic logging inside `zoomDiagnostics.js` without global console monkey-patching.
